source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the picture, three strips of the same horizontal width $a$ are marked 1,2,3. These strips connect the two parallel lines. Which strip has the biggest area?
<image1>
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: All three strips have the same area

choice_map.A: All three strips have the same area

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'In the picture' and refers to strips 1, 2, 3 and two parallel lines
the task depends on visual/spatial properties of diagram-defined regions rather than text alone
asset_registry_record shows image_manifest exists=true and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the problem asks to compare areas of three different diagram regions, which typically requires identifying each strip and reasoning about their geometric relationship
the answer 'All three strips have the same area' suggests comparison across all three regions rather than a single direct lookup
the prompt combines textual constraints ('same horizontal width a', 'connect the two parallel lines') with diagram interpretation
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true indicates upstream expectation of nontrivial reasoning potential
verifiability: choices are provided and answer_text exactly matches choice A
answer_type is short_text with a clear, discrete target outcome
because this is effectively multiple-choice, final-answer correctness is easy to check even if full geometric justification is harder

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产齐全，registry_passed=true，且无已登记问题
图像依赖度高但对应图像文件存在，可支撑核心语义恢复与后续处理
verifiability_score=0.93，答案为清晰的短文本且可与选项直接核验
multi_step_score=0.66，具备一定推理价值，适合进入候选池
虽有 diagram_interpretation_required 风险，但当前并不存在核心资产缺失，不足以降为 low_priority
